title: No explicit (env ...) override form for child processes that legitimately need a scrubbed LD_*/DYLD_* var
goal: lib/agent/c/agent_subprocess.c's scrubbed-child-environment builder (audit C6) filters dynamic-linker injection vars (LD_PRELOAD, DYLD_INSERT_LIBRARIES, LD_AUDIT, LD_LIBRARY_PATH, DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH, DYLD_FRAMEWORK_PATH, LD_BIND_NOW) out of every child process's environment by default, which is the correct default (prevents lateral movement via a compromised shell profile injecting a library into every tool the agent launches). But there is currently no supported way for a caller that legitimately needs one of those vars for a specific child (e.g. a sanitizer harness that needs LD_PRELOAD for its own instrumentation) to opt back in — the comment marks this 'TODO once we wire env override', meaning today such a caller has no sanctioned path and would have to bypass the scrubbing entirely or hand-roll process spawning.
next_actions: Design an explicit, opt-in (env ("LD_PRELOAD" . "/path/to/lib.so") ...) form on the Scheme subprocess-spawning API that re-adds specific scrubbed vars for that one call only (never silently for all children).
Wire it through to the scrubbed-env builder in agent_subprocess.c so an explicit override always wins over the default scrub list, but the default (no override) stays fully scrubbed.

Add tests/agent/subprocess_env_override_test.esk asserting: (a) default subprocess spawn has LD_PRELOAD stripped even if set in the parent env; (b) explicit (env ...) override on a spawn call restores it for that child only; (c) sibling/subsequent spawns without the override remain scrubbed.
acceptance: A subprocess spawned without any override never receives LD_PRELOAD/DYLD_INSERT_LIBRARIES/etc even if the parent process has them set.
A subprocess spawned WITH an explicit (env ...) override receives exactly the overridden vars, nothing more.
No change in default behavior for any existing caller that doesn't use the new override form.
ux_payoff: Closes the one documented escape hatch gap in the C6 environment-scrubbing hardening — without it, a legitimate advanced use case has no sanctioned path and might otherwise motivate someone to weaken the default scrub instead.
